A GitHub issue asks to document a Mojo import workaround
A module docstring is missing guidance for users who cannot import types from the parent package

name: mojo-moveinit-list-corruption
trigger_phrases: Struct move breaks assertions on List-derived data
Considering switching .copy() to ^ in __moveinit__ for performance
Diagnosing Mojo 0.26.1 bug where ^ transfer of List in __moveinit__ corrupts in-place-mutated values

name: mojo-multidim-setitem-tests
trigger_phrases: Adding stride-aware multi-dim assignment (t[i,j]=v) to a Mojo tensor
Writing 2D/3D __setitem__ tests verifying flat index = sum(idx*stride)
Implementing variadic-Int __setitem__ on ExTensor

name: mojo-narrowing-cast-tests
trigger_phrases: Adding UInt narrowing tests for a new target type
Extending test_unsigned.mojo with new cast coverage
Documents how to add narrowing conversion tests in Mojo following the if/raise pattern

name: mojo-nd-slice-copy-semantics
trigger_phrases: N-D tensor slice tests are skipped/disabled
__getitem__(*slices) returns wrong values for 2D/3D/ND slices
Multi-dim slicing uses self.copy() + data pointer offset which fails for non-contiguous results

name: mojo-nd-slice-fast-path
trigger_phrases: Optimizing batch-extraction in training loops
Adding a contiguous-memcpy fast-path for N-D tensor slices
Only the first axis is non-trivially sliced

name: mojo-nested-fn-capture-fix
trigger_phrases: Getting cannot synthesize fieldwise init because field has non-copyable and non-movable type errors
Mojo fieldwise init synthesis failure when nested fn captures non-copyable struct
Nested fn capture triggers non-movable type errors

name: mojo-noncontiguous-reshape-fix
trigger_phrases: Debugging wrong element order after transpose/slice
Implementing copy loops that must respect non-C-order layouts
Flat-index bugs in Mojo tensor ops that ignore strides on non-contiguous inputs

name: mojo-note-to-docstring
trigger_phrases: Converting inline # NOTE: implementation-constraint comments to proper docstring Note: sections
Condensing verbose compiler-limitation NOTEs with version + tracking references on GLIBC-mismatched systems
Enforcing # NOTE (Mojo vX.Y.Z): format compliance via pre-commit hook and Python audit script

name: mojo-numerical-gradient-test
trigger_phrases: Adding backward pass gradient tests
Avoiding grad_output=ones cancellation
Verifying analytical vs numerical gradient agreement

name: mojo-overload-ambiguity-typed-tensor-isolation
trigger_phrases: Getting cannot implicitly convert AnyTensor to AnyTensor errors
Adding typed Tensor[dtype] overloads alongside AnyTensor functions
Mojo compiler fails to resolve between two function signatures that both accept AnyTensor

name: mojo-overload-disambiguation-with-is-defined
trigger_phrases: Adding a convenience overload that auto-detects a parameter via compile-time is_defined[]
Hitting ambiguous call errors with two overloads sharing leading parameters with defaults
Detecting Apple Silicon or platform traits at compile time

name: mojo-package-level-reexports
trigger_phrases: Enabling from shared import X style imports
Activating commented-out import lines after implementation is ready
Adding optimizer structs (AdaGrad/RMSprop/AdamW)

name: mojo-param-gradient-check
trigger_phrases: A backward pass returns multiple gradients but only input gradients are tested
Adding gradient checks for normalization layer parameters
Numerical gradient validation for learnable parameters (gamma, beta) in Mojo backward passes

name: mojo-param-method-collision
trigger_phrases: CI fails with invalid redefinition on a parametric struct method
A trait requires fn dtype() but the struct has [dtype: DType] parameter
Typed overloads create no matching function ambiguity with existing untyped functions
